About

rockclimb

I am an evolutionary ecologist. My research focuses on how ecological and evolutionary processes jointly shape species geographical distributions and community structure, particularly in an era of global change.

I use a combination of experimental evolution, field work, and data synthesis approaches to test how the interaction between population genetics and ecology scales up to govern: (1) limits and changes to species distributions; (2) spatio-temporal patterns of species coexistence; (3) and evolutionary responses to warming in ecological communities. My research integrates themes and frameworks from evolutionary biology, population ecology, and community ecology.

I am currently an Ecology & Evolutionary Biology Postdoctoral Fellow at the University of Toronto, working with Megan Frederickson and Stephen Wright. I will be starting as an Assistant Professor at Yale University in 2027. Stay tuned for more!
